versions.label: BSB
paragraphs: 19 Have You rejected Judah completely?
Do You despise Zion?
Why have You stricken us
so that we are beyond healing?
We hoped for peace,
but no good has come,
and for the time of healing,
but there was only terror.
20 We acknowledge our wickedness, O LORD,
the guilt of our fathers;
indeed, we have sinned against You.
21 For the sake of Your name do not despise us;
do not disgrace Your glorious throne.
Remember Your covenant with us;
do not break it.
22 Can the worthless idols of the nations bring rain?
Do the skies alone send showers?
Is this not by You, O LORD our God?
So we put our hope in You,
for You have done all these things.

context_lines: In **Jeremiah 14**, this section should be read within the flow of the chapter and the larger movement of Jeremiah.
common_interpretation_lines: A common reading of **A Prayer for Mercy** is that it should be interpreted in light of the chapter's full narrative movement rather than as an isolated proof text.
Where interpreters differ on details, the immediate setting in **Jeremiah 14** remains the first control on meaning.
